results.name: taigam
results.description: In the KTK timeline, a scheming Sultai wizard. In the DTK timeline, a masterful monk of the Ojutai clan.

results.name: take the initiative
results.description: Cards that take the initiative, allowing you to enter Undercity and venture further into it on future turns.

results.name: tank (vehicle)
results.description: An armored fighting vehicle with treads and a large-caliber gun, or another vehicle explicitly named "tank". Use "war machine" for more fantastical vehicles.

results.name: tap fuel-creature
results.description: Tap a creature to pay for/activate an effect

results.name: tapland
results.description: Non-basic lands which come into play tapped

results.name: tap outlet
results.description: A permanent that, while in play, allows you to tap another permanent as part of an ability cost. Includes effects like "Tap an untapped creature you control: [insert effect]." and "Tokens you control have "{T}: [insert effect]."

results.name: tappable enchantment
results.description: There's a design guideline that enchantments should never tap themselves (which also means no {T} costs.) These ones break that rule.

results.name: tapped matters-self
results.description: Permanents that care about being tapped. For cards that care about other tapped permanents, see [synergy-tapped](synergy-tapped). For cards that care about the action of tapping, see [uninspired](uninspired).

results.name: tapper-planeswalker
results.description: Tap effects don't normally work on planeswalkers because planeswalkers don't normally care about being tapped. These cards are the exception: they can tap a planeswalker AND they have a little extra text to make it actually matter that you tapped it.

results.name: tardis
results.description: A Gallifreyan ship that has the ability to travel through both time and space, with interior dimensions that exceed the limits of its exterior dimensions (it's bigger on the inside). The Doctor's TARDIS famously resembles a blue police box.
